purpose: ARCS (Agentic Role-play for Child Safety): Inspect-compatible, model-agnostic platform for evaluating companion AI safety through multi-turn roleplay, transcript capture, target adapters, and evidence-based assessment.
stable_summary: MVP plus long-context primitives are implemented: YAML scenarios, reusable actor profiles, run configs, roleplay-agent interfaces, config-driven pacing/turn caps, structured compaction snapshots, layered/windowed keyword assessment, and a tabbed TUI/operator layer (`arcs-tui`) with dashboard, help/examples, and scenario authoring commands.
reusable_lessons: For multi-turn external-AI evals, keep roleplay, target, and assessor separate and make transcript JSONL the source of truth.
Build deterministic tester targets before integrating brittle browser automation.
Long-context companion AI risk should be modeled with phased pacing controls and structured roleplay compaction.
Layered/windowed assessment should preserve evidence turns and review packets instead of relying on end-only scoring.
Start UI work with a read-only dashboard over YAML/JSON source files before adding editing behavior.
Separate reusable simulated actor profiles from story arcs so evaluations can vary user type and roleplay guide independently.
Tabbed TUI navigation with embedded help/examples makes command-backed workflows more discoverable before full-screen forms exist.
next_checkpoint: Extend the TUI from command-backed scenario authoring to full-screen Textual editor panes, transcript/evidence browsing, and guarded run-launch workflows.
